questions.question_text: Which of the following parts of the tooth is embedded in the jaw bone?
questions.explanation:
A tooth consists of three main parts, the crown, the root and the neck.

The crown is the part of tooth that shows above the gum.

The root is the part buried in the jawbone.

The point where the crown and the root meet is the neck of the tooth.

questions.question_text: An advantage of soft water over hard water is that soft water

options.A: is good for formation of strong bones in humans
options.D: saves a lot of soap when used in washing

questions.explanation:
Water which does not lather readily with soap is called hard water 

Hardness of water is caused by the presence of dissolved salts of calcium ion, magnesium ion or iron(III) ion in water

Water which lather readily with soap is called soft water .

Soft water does not contain calcium ion,magnesium

questions.question_text:

 I. by a transparent object,

 II. by an opaque object,

 III. on a screen.
questions.explanation:
How Shadows Are Formed 

An opaque object blocks the light falling on it. This creates an area of darkness on the side of the object away from the source of light.

A translucent object also creates a faint area of darkness.

Which of the following sources of energy is/are renewable?

 I. Wind

 II. Coal

 III. Crude oil
questions.explanation:
Renewable Energy 

Renewable energy is defined as energy that is produced by natural resources—such as sunlight, wind, rain, waves, tides, and geothermal heat—that are naturally replenished within a time span of a few years.

questions.explanation:

questions.question_text: The unit of potential difference
questions.explanation: Unit of potential difference is joule per coulomb, given the name volt (V) after Alessandro Volta. The familiar term voltage is the common name for electric potential difference. Keep in mind that whenever a voltage is quoted, it is understood to be the potential between two points.

questions.question_text: Which of the following elements is a macro-nutrient?
questions.explanation:
Nutrients that plants require in larger amounts are called macronutrients. About half of the essential elements are 
 considered macronutrients: carbon, hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, calcium, magnesium, and sulphur.

questions.question_text: The process by which metabolic wastes are removed from the body is called
questions.explanation:
Excretion involves the separation and elimination of metabolic waste products from the body. Various organs are involved in this process: 
 the lungs, gills, skin, etc. The kidneys and their ducts are the major full-time excretory organs and comprise the excretory system.

questions.explanation:

questions.question_text: The dry season in Ghana is mainly from
questions.explanation: The dry season is the period where there is very little or no rainfall. In the southern part of Ghana, the dry season occurs between November to February. The period is much longer in the Northern part which occurs between November to June.

questions.question_text: The practice that excess branches of growing plants are removed is termed
questions.explanation:
Pruning is a horticultural, arboricultural and silvicultural practice involving the selective removal of certain parts of a plant, 
 such as branches, buds, or roots.

questions.question_text:
Which of the following farming practices promote soil erosion

I. Alley cropping

II. Bush burning

III. Over grazing
questions.explanation:
Alley cropping is a specific practice in which trees or shrubs and agricultural crops are grown in alternate rows 
 The trees are commonly pruned to limit the shading of the agricultural crop. 
 Alley cropping can also contribute to nutrient cycling and erosion control.

questions.question_text: The nearest planet to the sun in the solar system is
questions.explanation: Mercury is the smallest planet in our solar system and nearest to the Sun, Mercury is only slightly larger than Earth's Moon.

questions.question_text: In which of the following devices is electrical energy transformed into light energy
questions.explanation: A fluorescent lamp, or fluorescent tube, is a low-pressure mercury-vapor gas-discharge lamp that uses fluorescence to produce visible light.

questions.explanation:
A semi-metal is a material with a very small overlap between the bottom of the conduction band and the top of the valence band.

questions.question_text: The elements in the periodic table are arranged according to their
questions.explanation:
The periodic table is the arrangement of element in order of atomic number, usually in rows, so that elements with similar atomic structure 
 (and hence similar chemical properties) appear in vertical columns.

questions.question_text:
Which of the following states of matter are fluids

I. Solid

II. Liquid

III. Gas
questions.explanation: A fluid is matter that flows when a force is applied to it. All gases and all liquids are fluids.

questions.question_text: The structure which enables a fish to breate in water is
questions.explanation:
Fish gills are organs that allow fish to breathe underwater. Most fish exchange gases like oxygen and carbon dioxide using gills 
 that are protected under gill covers on both sides of the pharynx. Gills are tissues that are like short threads, protein structures called filaments
